title: Fix BsMatch GC root tracking and heap-before-alloc dangle risks
purpose: binary_allocation_roots returns empty Vec for BsMatch — match context not rooted during nested get_binary/get_tail allocations that trigger GC. Also jit_bs_finish reads builder bytes into a local slice then allocates (GC may relocate source). Same pattern in map_update. These are latent use-after-free bugs that manifest under GC pressure during binary operations.
task: Fix GC root tracking for BsMatch opcode in ir_binary.rs so that binary_allocation_roots returns the match context and all nested destination operands. Fix jit_bs_finish and map_update in runtime.rs to copy source data to stack-local storage before any allocation that could trigger GC. Add test coverage that validates safepoint root sets for binary opcodes.

requirements.title: BsMatch binary_allocation_roots must include match context and nested destinations
requirements.spec: WHEN the JIT compiler emits a GC safepoint for a BsMatch opcode, THE SYSTEM SHALL include the match_context operand and all nested destination operands in the root set returned by binary_allocation_roots. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT return an empty Vec from binary_allocation_roots for BsMatch when the opcode contains allocating sub-operations (get_binary, get_tail).
acceptance: binary_allocation_roots(BsMatch { match_context, commands }) returns a Vec containing match_context
binary_allocation_roots(BsMatch { .. }) returns all destination operands from nested get_binary and get_tail commands
Returned Vec is non-empty when BsMatch contains allocating sub-operations
Test: BsMatch with get_binary sub-op includes match_context in safepoint root set
Test: BsMatch with get_tail sub-op includes both match_context and destination in root set

requirements.title: Fix jit_bs_finish dangle — copy bytes before allocation
requirements.spec: WHEN jit_bs_finish completes binary construction, THE SYSTEM SHALL copy the builder's accumulated bytes into a stack-local Vec before calling allocate_binary. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT hold a reference to builder bytes across any heap allocation call, because GC may relocate the builder's backing storage.
acceptance: jit_bs_finish copies builder.bytes() into a local Vec<u8> before allocate_binary
No slice or reference to builder storage is live across the allocate_binary call
Test: jit_bs_finish produces correct binary when allocation triggers GC (simulated via small heap)

requirements.title: Fix map_update dangle — copy entries before allocation
requirements.spec: WHEN map_update constructs a new map from existing entries, THE SYSTEM SHALL copy map key-value entries into stack-local storage before calling write_map_entries or any other function that allocates on the process heap. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT hold references to source map entries across any heap allocation call.
acceptance: map_update copies source entries into local storage before any heap allocation
No reference to source map backing storage is live across a heap allocation call
Test: map_update produces correct map when allocation triggers GC (simulated via small heap)

requirements.title: Test coverage for BsMatch safepoint root correctness
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL include integration tests that verify BsMatch safepoint root sets contain the match context. THE SYSTEM SHALL test that a BsMatch with multiple allocating sub-operations (e.g., get_binary followed by get_tail) roots all intermediate and final operands.
acceptance: Test: BsMatch safepoint root set includes match_context for a single get_binary sub-op
Test: BsMatch safepoint root set includes match_context and both destinations for get_binary + get_tail
Test: non-allocating BsMatch sub-ops (get_integer, get_float with fixed size) do not add spurious roots

requirements.title: SHALL NOT return empty roots for allocating binary opcodes
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT return an empty Vec from binary_allocation_roots for any binary opcode that performs heap allocation (BsMatch with get_binary/get_tail, BsPutBinary, BsPutString). WHEN binary_allocation_roots is called for a non-allocating binary opcode, THE SYSTEM SHALL return an empty Vec.
acceptance: binary_allocation_roots returns non-empty Vec for BsMatch containing get_binary
binary_allocation_roots returns non-empty Vec for BsMatch containing get_tail
binary_allocation_roots returns empty Vec for BsMatch containing only get_integer with fixed size
No allocating binary opcode path returns an empty root set

boundaries: SHALL NOT change the extern "C" signatures of any runtime helper functions
SHALL NOT modify GC safepoint emission logic — only the root sets fed to existing safepoint infrastructure
SHALL NOT fix dangle patterns in code paths outside jit_bs_finish and map_update (other patterns are out of scope)
SHALL NOT change binary matching semantics — only root tracking and allocation ordering
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
binary_allocation_roots returns non-empty Vec for BsMatch with allocating sub-ops
jit_bs_finish does not hold builder slice across allocation
map_update does not hold source references across allocation
